non_claims: no persisted per-file content Merkle tree in AEGIS-Q
no replacement claim for kernel integrity mechanisms
scope: Non-numeric integrity-oriented comparison.  The current Thor kernel does not expose fs-verity or dm-integrity, and their protection/update models are not matched throughput baselines for mutable AEGIS-Q FUSE records.

systems.protection_boundary: Per-file read-only authenticity for file contents using a Merkle tree whose root is supplied to the filesystem.
systems.update_model: Files become immutable after verity is enabled; mutable overwrite, journal/checkpoint publication, and SQLite-style updates are not the same workload.
systems.why_no_throughput_number: A fio overwrite/fdatasync number would measure an invalid update model: fs-verity protects sealed read-only files, while AEGIS-Q evaluates mutable encrypted records.

systems.protection_boundary: Block-device integrity tags below the filesystem, optionally composed with dm-crypt/LUKS authenticated encryption.
systems.update_model: Block-level tag updates and dm-integrity journal or bitmap policy; no per-file envelope, checkpoint, or application oracle semantics.
systems.why_no_throughput_number: A number would depend on unavailable kernel support and on a chosen tag/journal mode, and would not isolate AEGIS-Q's per-file mutable FUSE publication protocol.

systems.protection_boundary: Per-file encrypted record format with AEAD tags, HMAC-authenticated envelope/checkpoint metadata, and optional external freshness anchor.
systems.update_model: Mutable FUSE reads/writes with data-before-journal/checkpoint publication; selected recovery states use explicit application oracles.
systems.why_no_throughput_number: The existing AEGIS-Q throughput row is a mutable secure-storage result, not an integrity-only comparison against read-only fs-verity or block-tag dm-integrity.
